title: Shape the React workspace shell for session operations
summary: Turned the dashboard into a task-first workspace shell so session inspection, queue state, and conflict visibility live in one React surface.
goal: Establish the main TSX layout for a frontend control plane where session operations can grow without the page collapsing into one long feed.
changesMade: Split the page into workspace bands for sessions, peers, conflicts, and files
Moved the page framing into dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx so the route stays thin
Normalized the top-level data contract that page.tsx passes into the shell
Reserved room in globals.css for denser frontend task surfaces and status chips

features.name: workspace shell frame
features.why: Give React frontend operators one stable home for session work instead of a stack of disconnected cards.

features.name: session-first navigation bands
features.why: Let the shell expand into a manager surface without reworking the app layout every time a new pane is added.

features.name: frontend dataset handoff contract
features.why: Keep the route loader lightweight while the TSX shell handles most presentation detail.

decisions.decision: Keep the route as a thin loader and push layout logic into TSX components
decisions.why: The frontend will iterate faster if page.tsx mostly handles data fetching and leaves composition to React components.

decisions.decision: Use bands and tabs instead of a single scrolling report
decisions.why: Operators need to switch between live concerns quickly without losing where they were in the interface.

openQuestions: Should the workspace shell pin the current session selection in the URL for sharable review links?
Do we want a compact density mode for heavy session corpora?
testsRun: manual browser smoke for dashboard shell
resumePrompt: Continue the workspace shell by checking dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx first, then extend page.tsx only if the data contract needs to grow.
